revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:群集墳]]
'''Clustered Kofuns''' (群集墳) are tightly concentrated [[Kofun cluster|Kofun groups]] from the late [[Kofun period]] to the final stages of the era. They consist of small-scale [[Kofuns]] (primarily [[round Kofuns]] and, rarely, [[square Kofuns]]), densely clustered within a limited area. Additionally, structures with mounds are prevalent, but even [[yokoana tombs]] are categorized under clustered Kofuns.
They are different from {{ill|Kofun cluster|ja|古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun cluster|lt=Kofun clusters}} because they are all made at the same time for a group of related individuals such as a kin group of a lord and his subordinates.
== Overview ==
Clustered Kofuns reached their peak during the late Kofun period, often constructed on hillsides or in relatively small spaces on [[:en:uplands|uplands]]. Kofuns were closely positioned, rarely separated by [[:en:Moats|Moats]] or [[dry moats]], and individual mounds were seldom named. Consequently, the entire group of mounds is referred to collectively, such as {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11502665|lt=Shinzawa Senozuka Kofun Group|1=Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} or {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11475228|lt=Iwasaka Senozuka Kofun Group|1=Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}. Individual mounds are numbered (e.g., {{ill|Shinzawa No. 126 Kofun|ja|新沢126号古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzawa No. 126 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504486|1=Shinzawa No. 126 Kofun}}), while larger mounds within the group may have specific names like "Tennozuka" or "Shogunzuka" in the Iwasaka Senozuka group.
The {{ill|Takayasu Senozuka Kofun Group|ja|高安千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayasu Senozuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504492|1=Takayasu Senozuka Kofun Group}} in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|qid=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}} is confirmed to contain 155 Kofuns. Additionally, the {{ill|Hirao Senozuka Kofun Group|ja|平尾山千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Senozuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504497|1=Hirao Senozuka Kofun Group}} in {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}} has over 1,000 Kofuns, illustrating a dramatic increase in the number of people capable of constructing Kofuns over time.
Initial clustered Kofuns from the 5th century, although smaller, featured burial facilities such as [[vertical stone chambers]], [[clay coffins]], or direct burial in [[Wooden Coffin|Wooden coffins]]. They also included grave goods like {{ill|Iron|en|Iron|qid=Q677|lt=Iron|1=Iron}} [[:en:weapons|weapons]] and [[agricultural tools]], similar to those in earlier periods.
The timeline of appearance, formation, and cessation varies. While some groups began in the 5th century and continued until the late 6th century, others started forming anew in the 7th century. Burial methods range widely, from wooden coffin direct burials to 5th-century [[horizontal stone chambers]]. The use of horizontal stone chambers became predominant after the mid-6th century. Grave goods also reflect diversity, ranging from weapon-dominated offerings to those centered on agricultural tools, indicating the social characteristics of the communities that created these mounds.
== Density of Kofuns ==
The diameter of the mounds in clustered burial groups typically ranges from 10 to 20 meters. Neighboring mounds are so close that their bases often touch, resulting in the absence of [[:en:Moats|Moats]] or [[:en:embankments|embankments]]. Clustered Kofuns are divided into smaller units known as "sub-clusters," which collectively form larger Kofun groups.
For instance, the Takayasu Senozuka Kofun Group originally consisted of about 500 mounds, covering an area approximately 500 meters east-west and 400 meters north-south. This area is comparable to the space occupied by a single colossal mound such as {{ill|Konda-yama Kofun|ja|誉田山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda-yama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11631432}} (modern-day Emperor Ojin’s Mausoleum).
== Major Clustered Kofuns ==
* {{ill|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|en|Yoshimi Hundred Caves|qid=Q11414302|lt=Yoshimi Hundred Caves|1=Yoshimi Hundred Caves}} ({{ill|Yoshimi, Saitama|en|Yoshimi, Saitama|qid=Q543313|lt=Yoshimi, Saitama|1=Yoshimi, Saitama}}, Saitama Prefecture) * {{ill|Manda Kuma-no-dai Tomb Cluster|ja|万田熊之台横穴群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manda Kuma-no-dai Tomb Cluster|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504499|1=Manda Kuma-no-dai Tomb Cluster}} ({{ill|Hiratsuka|en|Hiratsuka|qid=Q502199|lt=Hiratsuka, Kanagawa|1=Hiratsuka}}, Kanagawa Prefecture) * {{ill|Kawada Kofun Group|ja|川田古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawada Kofun Group|1=Kawada Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97196199}} ({{ill|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|en|Nakanoto, Ishikawa|qid=Q1349436|lt=Nakanoto, Ishikawa|1=Nakanoto, Ishikawa}} and {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|qid=Q695892|lt=Nanao, Ishikawa|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}}, Ishikawa Prefecture) * Funakiyama Kofun Group (Northwest {{ill|Gifu|en|Gifu|qid=Q45798|lt=Gifu City|1=Gifu}}, over 200 mounds) * Ganseiji Kofun Group ({{ill|Ibi River|en|Ibi River|qid=Q1204160|lt=Ibi River|1=Ibi River}} area, Gifu Prefecture, over 100 mounds) * Akasaka Kofun Group (Ibi River area, Gifu Prefecture, over 100 mounds) * {{ill|Kobata-yama Kofun Group|ja|木幡山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobata-yama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504503|1=Kobata-yama Kofun Group}} ({{ill|Uji|en|Uji|qid=Q326956|lt=Uji, Kyoto|1=Uji}}, South Yamashiro’s largest group with 120 small round Kofuns, including {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara Clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} graves) * {{ill|Kuwabara Clustered Tombs|ja|桑原遺跡群集墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuwabara Clustered Tombs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504505|1=Kuwabara Clustered Tombs}} ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|en|Ibaraki, Osaka|qid=Q502403|lt=Ibaraki, Osaka|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture) * {{ill|Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Cluster|lt=Nagahara Kofun Group|ja|長原古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahara Kofun Group|qq=}} ({{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}}, Osaka Prefecture) * {{ill|Hirao Senozuka Kofun Group|ja|平尾山千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirao Senozuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504497|1=Hirao Senozuka Kofun Group}} (Kashiwara City, Osaka Prefecture, approximately 600 mounds, late period) * {{ill|Karindobata Kofun Group|ja|雁多尾畑古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karindobata Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504507|1=Karindobata Kofun Group}} ({{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}, approximately 400 mounds, horizontal stone chambers, late period) * {{ill|Wani Clustered Tombs|ja|和爾群集墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wani Clustered Tombs|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504509|1=Wani Clustered Tombs}} ({{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Wani, Nara Prefecture) * {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11502665|lt=Shinzawa Senozuka Kofun Group|1=Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} ({{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|qid=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, early clustered Kofuns from the 5th century) * {{ill|Yamaguchi Senozuka Kofun Group|ja|山口千塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Senozuka Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504512|1=Yamaguchi Senozuka Kofun Group}} and {{ill|Teraguchi Shinomi Kofun Group|ja|寺口忍海古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Teraguchi Shinomi Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504514|1=Teraguchi Shinomi Kofun Group}} ({{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|qid=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi, Nara|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}) * {{ill|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11475228|lt=Iwakura Kofun Group|1=Iwase-Senzuka Kofun Cluster}} ({{ill|Wakayama (city)|en|Wakayama (city)|qid=Q200747|lt=Wakayama, Wakayama|1=Wakayama (city)}}, Wakayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Seionmura Touge Kofun Group|ja|清音村峠古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seionmura Touge Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504517|1=Seionmura Touge Kofun Group}} ({{ill|Kiyone, Okayama|en|Kiyone, Okayama|qid=Q11561455|lt=Kiyone Village|1=Kiyone, Okayama}}, Okayama Prefecture) * {{ill|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|en|Saitobaru Kofun Cluster|qid=Q3469314|lt=Saitobaru Kofun Group|1=Saitobaru Kofun Cluster}} (Miyazaki Prefecture)
